# backupgram

> A pure-Bash Docker image that runs automated PostgreSQL backups on a cron
> schedule, with rotating retention, Telegram delivery (Bot API + MTProto),
> optional GPG encryption, webhooks, multi-chat fan-out, auto-discovery of
> databases, an optional HTTP REST control API, and built-in restore tooling.
> Published as `ganiyevuz/backupgram:<pg-version>[-alpine]`.

## Core Files
- [scripts/init.sh]: ENTRYPOINT — validates config, then execs go-cron with SCHEDULE
- [scripts/env.sh]: dual-purpose config — sourced for validation/var resolution, executed standalone at startup
- [scripts/backup.sh]: the backup cycle — dump, verify, encrypt, rotate, send to Telegram, prune
- [scripts/restore.sh]: restore tooling — interactive, by file, cross-database, or --from-telegram
- [tg-upload/]: Go binary (gotd/td) for MTProto upload/download of files up to 2 GB
- [rest-api/]: Go module (net/http) for the optional REST control API; layered packages (httpx/config/backups/jobs/handlers/server/supervisor)
- [hooks/]: run-parts hooks (pre-backup | post-backup | error); 00-webhook implements WEBHOOK_* vars
- [docker/debian.Dockerfile, docker/alpine.Dockerfile]: image definitions; kept in lockstep (identical ENV + symlinks)
- [generate-docker-bake.sh]: source of truth for docker-bake.hcl (generated — do not hand-edit)
- [.github/workflows/ci.yml]: runs scripts against a live postgres:16, then builds/publishes images

## Documentation
- [README.md]: quick start, environment variables, CLI commands, how backups work, hooks
- [CHANGELOG.md]: notable changes (Keep a Changelog format)
- [docs/GETTING_STARTED.md]: zero-to-restorable-backup walkthrough and troubleshooting
- [docs/CONFIGURATION.md]: every environment variable, Docker secrets, retention math
- [docs/CLI.md]: backup, restore, list, status, help with example output
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md]: runtime chain, backup cycle, rotation model, format branches (C4 + mermaid)
- [docs/REST_API.md]: optional HTTP control surface — endpoints, bearer auth, runtime config editing
- [docs/BUILD.md]: multi-arch build with QEMU + buildx
- [docs/LARGE_FILES.md]: MTProto large-file (>50 MB, up to 2 GB) upload route
- [CLAUDE.md]: runtime architecture, build system, testing, and conventions for contributors

## Key Concepts
- Rotation model: each run writes to last/, then hard-links into daily/weekly/monthly/ (same inode, no extra disk); requires a POSIX filesystem with hardlink+symlink support
- Format branches: gzip SQL (.sql.gz, default), directory (-Fd), cluster (pg_dumpall), each optionally GPG-wrapped (.gpg) — kept in sync across backup.sh and restore.sh
- Telegram transport: TELEGRAM_UPLOAD_METHOD = smart | botapi | mtproto; 50 MB Bot API limit enforced only against the official api.telegram.org
- Multi-chat fan-out: comma-separated TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID; uploaded once, file_id reused per chat
- Restore by message id: backups embed a 🔖 Restore ID in their caption; restore --from-telegram fetches and restores it
- Secrets precedence: *_FILE (Docker secrets) takes precedence over the plain env var
- Scheduling/health: go-cron (prodrigestivill/go-cron) owns SCHEDULE and serves the healthcheck on HEALTHCHECK_PORT
- Auto-discover: POSTGRES_DB_AUTODISCOVER backs up every non-template DB (minus postgres and POSTGRES_DB_EXCLUDE); POSTGRES_DB optional; ignored in cluster mode; an empty discovered set aborts the run
- REST API: optional (REST_API_ENABLE) HTTP control surface behind a bearer token (REST_API_TOKEN); backupgram-api becomes PID 1 and supervises go-cron; long ops run as async jobs (202 + GET /jobs/{id}); listens on REST_API_PORT (8081)
